docs.title: Paper Replication
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for our replication of [Paper Title] (link to paper).

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Faithfully reproduce the key experiments and results from the original paper.
  • Maintain a clean, well-tested codebase so that overseers and collaborators can follow along.
  • Document deviations or improvements clearly.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# 1. Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/<your-org>/<your-repo>.git
cd <your-repo>

# 2. Install in editable mode with dev tools
pip install -e ".[dev,docs]"

# 3. Install pre-commit hooks
pre-commit install

# 4. Run tests
pytest

docs.title: Results
docs.text: Experiment Metric Paper Result Our Result Delta Exp 1 — — — —

docs.title: Notes & Observations
docs.text: Document surprises, dead ends, and hyperparameter choices here.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Provide a 2–3 paragraph summary of the paper's contribution here.

docs.title: Deviations from Original
docs.text: Document any intentional or forced differences here as the replication progresses.

docs.title: Progress Tracker
docs.text: This page is updated on each merge to main and is visible to overseers via GitHub Pages.

docs.title: Milestones
docs.text: # Milestone Status Notes 1 Repo setup & CI ✅ Done 2 Data pipeline 🔄 In progress 3 Baseline model ⬜ Pending 4 Experiment 1 ⬜ Pending 5 Results & analysis ⬜ Pending

docs.title: Notebooks
docs.text: Exploratory notebooks live in the notebooks/ folder at the repo root. They are rendered here automatically via mkdocs-jupyter.

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text:
• Notebooks call into src/paper_replication — keep heavy logic there, not in cells.
  • Never commit cell outputs. nbstripout runs as a pre-commit hook and strips them automatically.
  • Name notebooks with a number prefix so they sort logically: 01_data_exploration.ipynb, 02_baseline.ipynb, etc.
  • Add a Markdown cell at the top of each notebook with a title and one-line description.
